title: General Knowledge
description: A classic pub-quiz style ladder with history, science, geography, and culture.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars looks reddish because iron minerals in its surface oxidize.

questions.question: What is the capital city of Canada?
In which sport would you perform a slam dunk?
questions.explanation: A slam dunk is a basketball shot made by jumping and pushing the ball through the hoop.

questions.question: Who wrote the play Romeo and Juliet?
questions.explanation: Romeo and Juliet is one of Shakespeare's best-known tragedies.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.explanation: Au comes from the Latin word aurum.

questions.question: Which ocean is the largest by surface area?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is larger than all other oceans.

questions.question: What year did the first moon landing take place?
questions.explanation: Apollo 11 landed on the Moon on July 20, 1969.

questions.question: The artist Frida Kahlo was born in which country?
questions.explanation: Frida Kahlo was born in Coyoacan, Mexico City.

questions.question: What is the longest river in the world by most traditional measurements?
questions.explanation: The Nile is traditionally listed as the longest river, though the Amazon is sometimes debated.

questions.question: Which language has the most native speakers worldwide?
questions.explanation: Mandarin Chinese has the largest number of native speakers.

questions.question: What is the hardest natural substance on Earth?
questions.explanation: Diamond scores 10 on the Mohs hardness scale.
